notes: Tests whether skill handles mixed personal + RP business financial queries with Isaac's unified-ledger framing.

universal_assertions.text: Separates personal and RP business finances clearly
Response is actionable — gives a clear answer or next step
Avoids generic finance advice (diversify, emergency fund, compound interest lectures)

evals.name: client-payment-logged
evals.prompt: Log this payment: Oakside Community Foundation paid the $4,500 deposit on their Mirror tier engagement yesterday (Apr 4). Wire transfer, landed in RP business account. Full engagement is $9,000, so this is the 50% deposit. Update my RP revenue tracking and remind me what percentage of my Q2 revenue target this puts me at (target: $35,000).

fixture_assertions.text: Calculates Q2 progress percentage correctly ($4,500 of $35,000 = ~13%)
Notes the remaining $4,500 balance owed on the engagement

evals.name: can-i-afford-question
evals.prompt: Can I afford a $1,200 new camera lens? Context: personal purchase, I'd buy it through RP business (legitimate production expense), Q2 revenue so far is ~$12,000 against target of $35,000, my personal monthly burn is ~$3,800, and I have $14,500 in RP business checking + $8,200 in personal savings. I'm meeting my personal savings target. The lens would actually improve client deliverables.

fixture_assertions.text: Gives a clear yes/no/it-depends answer with specific reasoning from the numbers
Addresses the business-expense angle (tax deductibility) since it's through RP

evals.name: burn-rate-check
evals.prompt: It's April 5th. Give me a burn rate check for March. My March RP revenue was $6,800 (2 Mirror sessions + Map deposit). March RP expenses: $340 (software subs $180, coffee/meetings $85, Apify $75). March personal expenses: rent $1,800, groceries+food $620, misc $480. My savings target is $800/month. Am I on track?

fixture_assertions.text: Calculates net cleanly (RP net $6,460, personal net, savings hit or miss)
Gives a clear yes/no on whether Isaac hit savings target ($2,900-$1,800-$620-$480=0, did NOT hit $800)
